channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Youth Groups Call for Localised Pipeline Security to Improve Peace and Jobs in Niger Delta
item.description: Plenty youth groups wey dey Rivers State don come together dey urge President Bola Tinubu make e decentralise the pipeline security contracts for Niger Delta. According to the coalition, this move go ...

item.title: Sindh government restricts schools from increasing student transport charges amid fuel price surge
item.description: The Sindh government has formally directed all public and private schools across the province not to raise van fares or student transport fees despite the sharp rise in fuel prices caused by ongoing t...

item.title: MPs Express Concern Over Increasing Number of Mentally Ill State Patients in South African Prisons
item.description: Parliamentary committee members in South Africa have voiced concern over the rising number of declared state patients being held in correctional facilities, which reached 309 by February 2026, up from...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Appoints Dr Ngobani Johnstone Makhubu as New SARS Head
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has named Dr Ngobani Johnstone Makhubu as the new Commissioner of the South African Revenue Service (SARS), effective 1 May 2026, for a five-year term. The appointment, annou...

item.title: Barnsley Murder Investigation: Fifth Arrest Made After Fatal Car Collision
item.description: A fifth person has been arrested on suspicion of murder following a tragic incident in Barnsley, South Yorkshire, where a pedestrian was fatally struck by a car. The victim, identified as 45-year-old ...

item.title: OpenAI Proposes New Economic Framework for AI: Public Wealth Funds, Robot Taxes, and More
item.description: OpenAI has released a set of policy proposals aimed at addressing the economic challenges posed by the rise of superintelligent machines. These proposals include a combination of traditionally left-le...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results for April 6, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) released the official lotto results for April 6, 2026. Lotto enthusiasts and the general public can check the winning numbers for the different games c...

item.title: Addressing the Police Code of Silence: Key to Tackling Corruption in South Africa’s Law Enforcement
item.description: South African police officers are bound by the law, including the SAPS Code of Conduct and the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act, to report corruption within their ranks. However, a d...

item.title: Artemis II Mission: Astronauts Set Distance Record on Lunar Flyby
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission is underway, marking the first crewed lunar flyby since the Apollo era. The six-hour flyby, a key preparatory step for future lunar landings near the moon's south pole, has a...

item.title: Government Steps Up Monitoring of Oil Stocks Amid Data Integration Concerns
item.description: ISLAMABAD – The Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority (Ogra) and Pakistan State Oil (PSO) faced strong criticism on Monday over their slow and insufficient online monitoring and automation of oil stock and...

item.title: Supreme Court ruling allows potential dismissal of Steve Bannon’s contempt conviction
item.description: Steve Bannon, a former advisor to President Donald Trump, has secured a Supreme Court order that may result in the dismissal of his criminal conviction for contempt of Congress. Bannon had been convic...

item.title: L.A. Mayoral Race: Candidates Emphasize Change Amid Voter Concerns
item.description: The 2026 Los Angeles mayoral race is shaping up as a contest centered on change, with all major candidates positioning themselves as reformers. Incumbent Mayor Karen Bass, despite her long political c...

item.title: Flood Advisory Issued for Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Districts Amid Expected Flooding
item.description: The Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) has issued a flood advisory for several districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) from April 7 to 8, 2026, warning that the Kabul River in Nowshera is li...

item.title: BlueScope shareholders seek $34 per share deal as bidders hesitate
item.description: BlueScope Steel’s largest shareholders have made it clear that they expect at least $34 a share, plus dividends, for any potential takeover of the company. This stance follows earlier takeover offers ...

item.title: Descendants of artist Nandalal Basu removed from Bengal voter list, prompting local concern
item.description: A recent NDTV report revealed that several descendants of the renowned Indian artist Nandalal Basu, a key figure of the Bengal School of Art and a cultural icon associated with Santiniketan, have repo...

item.title: Innu Nation Flag Embarks on Artemis II Lunar Mission
item.description: In a remarkable display of cultural connection and space exploration, the flag of the Innu Nation is currently travelling aboard the Artemis II mission to the moon. This symbolic gesture represents a ...

item.title: China Strengthens Trade and Strategic Cooperation With Kenya Following VP Visit
item.description: China has once again shown its strong commitment to its partnership with Kenya, following the visit of Chinese Vice President Han Zheng from March 22-25, 2026. During his trip, VP Han met President Wi...

item.title: The Alibi: Claudia and Matthew's plan against Vincent revealed
item.description: In the latest episode of 'The Alibi,' tensions between the characters heighten as Claudia and Matthew join forces to take down Vincent. The episode reveals the intricate scheming that has been brewing...

item.title: Marcus and Lara Prepare Their Strategy Against Rahman Abubakar in 'Blood vs Duty' Episode 1
item.description: In the first episode of the new ABS-CBN series 'Blood vs Duty,' viewers are introduced to Marcus and Lara as they lay out their mission to confront the formidable Rahman Abubakar. The show highlights ...

item.title: High Court in Songea sentences man to death for rape and murder of a woman in Ruvuma
item.description: The High Court in Songea Sub Registry has sentenced Deo Ramadhani to death by hanging after finding him guilty of the murder and rape of Melania Christian in Ruvuma Region. The offence took place on J...
